title: What happens if I break a traffic rule?
explanation: Traffic rules exist to ensure the safety of all road users. Breaking them can result in penalties, fines, and in serious cases, legal action.
consequences: Fine as per Motor Vehicles Act
Criminal penalties for rash driving causing injury

title: What happens if my fundamental rights are violated?
explanation: Fundamental rights are guaranteed by the Constitution and protect every citizen. If violated, you have the right to seek judicial remedy through the courts.
consequences: Right to move Supreme Court for writs (Article 32)
Damages and compensation may be awarded
Government action can be declared void

title: What happens if I'm arrested without a warrant?
explanation: The Constitution provides protection against arbitrary arrest. Police cannot arrest anyone without proper legal procedure and reasonable cause.
consequences: Unlawful detention claim can be filed
Habeas corpus petition to challenge arrest

title: What happens if I'm discriminated against in public places?
explanation: The Constitution prohibits discrimination based on religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth. All public places must provide equal access to all citizens.
consequences: Right to move court for justice

title: What happens if I don't pay taxes?
explanation: Paying taxes is a civic duty and legal obligation for all eligible citizens and businesses. Non-payment results in penalties and legal action.

title: What happens if I'm denied the right to vote?
explanation: Every eligible citizen of India has the right to vote in elections. Denying someone this right is a violation of constitutional rights and can be challenged in court.
consequences: Court can issue directions to conduct elections
Compensation for denial of voting rights

title: What happens if I'm denied admission to a public school?
explanation: The right to education is a fundamental right, and no citizen can be denied admission based on religion, caste, sex, or place of birth. Educational institutions must provide equal opportunities.
consequences: Right to move court for admission
Institution may face penalties or suspension of recognition

title: What happens if I'm detained without informing my family?
explanation: The Constitution guarantees the right to information about detention. Authorities must inform your family members and provide access to a lawyer within specific timeframes.
consequences: Habeas corpus petition can be filed
Action against police officers for violation

title: What happens if my property is seized without compensation?
explanation: While the government can acquire property for public interest, proper notice must be given and fair compensation must be provided as per constitutional procedure.
consequences: Right to seek compensation in court

title: What happens if I'm not allowed to practice my religion?
explanation: The Constitution guarantees freedom of religion to all citizens. No one can be prevented from practicing their religion or performing religious rituals.
consequences: Right to move court for religious freedom
Court can issue injunctions against interference

title: What happens if I'm denied access to information from government?
explanation: The Right to Information Act gives all citizens the right to seek information from government bodies. Denial without valid reason is illegal.
consequences: Violation of Article 19 and RTI Act

title: What happens if I'm forced to work without wages?
explanation: The Constitution prohibits forced labor and begar (unpaid work). Every worker has the right to fair wages and safe working conditions.
consequences: Violation of Article 23 (prohibition of forced labor)
Right to file complaint with labor authorities
Court can award compensation and wages

title: What happens if I'm tortured in police custody?
explanation: Torture or cruel treatment by police is strictly prohibited and is a violation of constitutional rights. The state must protect the physical and mental dignity of all persons.
consequences: Case can be filed in Supreme Court

title: What happens if a child is denied right to free education?
explanation: Every child has the right to free and compulsory education up to age 14. This is a fundamental right and cannot be denied on any basis.
consequences: Parents can move court for admission
School can face action from education board
Right to compensation for denial of education

title: What happens if I'm wrongfully convicted of a crime?
explanation: If you are proved innocent after wrongful conviction, the Constitution provides remedies. You can seek compensation and rehabilitation from the state.
consequences: Right to file appeal and revision
Compensation under Article 33 or special provision
